skill.name: tavily-search
skill.description: Use the Tavily API for web search, current information retrieval, question answering, and topic research.
skill.summary: Search the web with Tavily for current information and research support.
skill.author: CK991357

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command execution, weak cryptography, and high entropy indicators in SKILL.md. Review found these are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, YAML metadata, and natural-language instructions, with no executable script, cryptographic implementation, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown fenced examples that show Tavily tool-call formatting. They are documentation text, not executable Ruby, shell, or command invocation logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are fenced JSON examples and explanatory bullets. No shell command, interpreter call, or user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Entropy Alerts Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert points to descriptive metadata, and the entropy alert points to the start of a short Markdown file. No hash algorithm, cipher use, binary blob, encoded payload, or encrypted content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain YAML front matter and a Chinese description. The file is readable Markdown documentation, so the high-risk indicators are unsupported by context.

content.value_statement: AI assistants need current information that model knowledge may not contain. This skill guides Tavily searches for timely research, fact checking, and topic background.
actual_capabilities: Shows the expected Tavily search tool-call structure.
Uses a query string parameter for web search requests.
Supports searches for current news and recent information.
Supports topic research and background information gathering.
Supports fact checking by searching for corroborating web information.
Encourages breaking complex questions into focused search queries.
limitations: Requires an available tavily_search tool and configured Tavily access.
Only documents query-based search behavior, not full API configuration.
Search quality depends on query wording and available web results.
Returned information still needs source review for accuracy.

use_cases.title: Track Current Developments
use_cases.description: Search recent web information about a company, event, technology, or public topic.

use_cases.title: Verify Time-Sensitive Claims
use_cases.description: Check whether a statement, price, release, or reported fact is supported by current sources.

use_cases.title: Build Topic Briefs
use_cases.description: Gather background information and recent context before writing a summary, memo, or report.

prompt_templates.title: Find Current Information
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Tavily for the latest information about [topic]. Summarize the most relevant points and note source freshness.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Specific Claim
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Tavily to verify this claim: [claim]. Compare current sources and explain whether the claim is supported.

prompt_templates.title: Research a Topic Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Tavily for recent and authoritative information about [topic]. Organize the findings into background, recent developments, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Run Multi-Query Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Break this research question into focused Tavily searches: [question]. Search each angle, compare results, and synthesize a concise answer.

output_examples.input: Search for recent OpenAI model release information.
output_examples.output: A concise summary of recent release details, source dates, and any unresolved differences between sources.

output_examples.input: Verify the current Bitcoin price trend.
output_examples.output: A short trend summary based on current search results, with a note that market data changes quickly.
A grouped explanation of major language, performance, and tooling updates found in web results.

best_practices: Use specific search terms and include dates, product names, or entities when relevant.
Split broad research tasks into several focused queries.
Review sources before treating search results as verified facts.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague one-word queries for complex research questions.
Do not treat a single search result as final proof.
Do not use this skill for private data lookup unless the data is publicly available.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an assistant to use Tavily search for current web information, fact checking, and topic research.

faq.question: Does it include the Tavily API key?
faq.answer: No. It assumes the tavily_search tool and any required Tavily credentials are already configured.

faq.question: Can it browse the web in real time?
faq.answer: It can request current web search through Tavily when the compatible tool is available.

faq.question: Is it useful for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Can it guarantee source accuracy?
faq.answer: No. It can retrieve search results, but users should review sources and compare evidence.

faq.question: Why was the audit risk level low?
faq.answer: Static alerts matched Markdown examples and metadata, not executable code or malicious behavior.
